content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian.Chef has an array AAA of size NNN. Chef wants to choose any subsequence of size **exactly** ‚ĆąN2‚ĆČ \lceil \frac{N}{2} \rceil‚Ćą2N‚Äč‚ĆČ from the array such that GCD of all the elements in that sequence must be 222. Chef names such a kind of sequence as a half-sequence.

Help Chef to find whether he would be able to select any half-sequence in the given array.

As a reminder,


A subsequence of an array is a sequence that can be derived from the given array by deleting zero or more elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.
GCD stands for Greatest Common Divisor. The greatest common divisor of a subsequence is the largest integer ddd such that all the numbers in sequence are divisible by ddd. For more information, refer to here.
‚Ćąx‚ĆČ\lceil x \rceil‚Ćąx‚ĆČ is the ceiling (round up) operation: ‚Ćą3.5‚ĆČ=4,‚Ćą2‚ĆČ=2\lceil 3.5 \rceil = 4 ,\lceil 2 \rceil = 2‚Ćą3.5‚ĆČ=4,‚Ćą2‚ĆČ=2.

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN denoting the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2....ANA_1, A_2....A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč....AN‚Äč denoting the given array.


Output Format
For each test case, output on one line `YES` if Chef can find a half-sequence, else print `NO`. Output is case insensitive.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, Chef wants to select ‚Ćą52‚ĆČ=3\lceil \frac{5}{2} \rceil = 3‚Ćą25‚Äč‚ĆČ=3 numbers. But for any 333 numbers, GCD would not be 222. Therefore the answer is NO.


For the second test case, Chef wants to select ‚Ćą42‚ĆČ=2\lceil \frac{4}{2} \rceil = 2‚Ćą24‚Äč‚ĆČ=2 numbers. Chef can select the subsequence [2,4][2,4][2,4] with GCD of 222. Therefore the answer is YES.


For the third test case, Chef wants to select ‚Ćą32‚ĆČ=2\lceil \frac{3}{2} \rceil = 2‚Ćą23‚Äč‚ĆČ=2 numbers. But for any 222 numbers, GCD would be bigger than 222. Therefore the answer is NO.
